core.label: Clojure Fundamentals
core.description: Setup, syntax, immutable data, and the REPL.

topics.label: Installation & REPL
topics.description: Install Clojure CLI tools and start the REPL. REPL-driven development is Clojure's primary workflow.

topics.label: Syntax & S-expressions
topics.description: Everything is a list — (function arg1 arg2). Prefix notation and homoiconicity (code is data).

topics.label: Data Types
topics.description: Numbers, strings, keywords, symbols, booleans, nil. Keywords (:name) are commonly used as map keys.

topics.label: Immutable Collections
topics.description: Lists, vectors, maps, and sets — all immutable and persistent. Structural sharing keeps them efficient.

topics.label: Functions
topics.description: defn, fn, and #() anonymous functions. Functions are the primary unit of abstraction.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if, when, cond, case, and do. All control structures are expressions that return values.

topics.label: Sequences & Laziness
topics.description: map, filter, reduce, take, drop on lazy sequences. Most operations are lazy by default.

topics.label: Destructuring
topics.description: Extract values from vectors and maps in let, fn, and defn. Powerful nested destructuring.

topics.label: Threading Macros
topics.description: -> and ->> for readable data pipelines. Thread-first and thread-last macros.

topics.label: Namespaces
topics.description: Organize code with ns, require, use, and import. Namespaces map to files.

core.label: State, Concurrency & Interop
core.description: Managed state, concurrency primitives, and Java interop.

topics.label: Atoms
topics.description: Managed mutable state with atom, swap!, reset!, deref/@. Atoms are thread-safe.

topics.label: Refs & STM
topics.description: Coordinated state changes with refs, dosync, alter. Software Transactional Memory.

topics.label: Agents
topics.description: Asynchronous state changes with send and send-off. Agents handle independent state updates.

topics.label: core.async
topics.description: CSP-style concurrency with channels, go blocks, and alt!. Clojure's answer to Go channels.

topics.label: Java Interop
topics.description: Call Java methods (.method obj), create instances (Class.), and import classes.

topics.label: Protocols & Records
topics.description: Define interfaces with defprotocol and implementations with defrecord. Polymorphism in Clojure.

topics.label: Multimethods
topics.description: Open dispatch on arbitrary criteria with defmulti and defmethod. More flexible than protocols.

topics.label: Specs
topics.description: Describe data shapes and function contracts with clojure.spec. Runtime validation and generative testing.

core.label: Tooling & Testing
core.description: deps.edn, Leiningen, testing, and project management.

topics.label: deps.edn & CLI
topics.description: Manage dependencies and run programs with the Clojure CLI and deps.edn.

topics.label: Leiningen
topics.description: Build automation with lein — the traditional Clojure build tool.

topics.label: Testing (clojure.test)
topics.description: Write tests with deftest, testing, is, and are. Built-in testing framework.

topics.label: Macros
topics.description: Write code that writes code with defmacro. Macros transform forms at compile time.

topics.label: Error Handling
topics.description: try/catch/finally and ex-info/ex-data for structured exceptions.

topics.label: Transducers
topics.description: Composable algorithmic transformations without intermediate collections. High-performance data processing.
